results.rendered_html:
huta p. p. [hu-kta]
1 Offered as an oblation to fire, burnt as a sacrificial offering.
2 One to whom an oblation is offered;  Ś. 4 ;  R. 2. 71. 
— taḥ N. of Śiva.
— taṃ An oblation, offering.
Comp.
— agni a. who has made an oblation to fire;  R. 1. 6.  (
— m.) a sacrificial fire.
— aśanaḥ
1 fire; samīraṇo nodayitā bhaveti vyādiśyate kena hutāśanasya  Ku. 3. 21 ,  R. 4. 1. 
2 N. of Śiva.
3 the Chitraka tree. ˚sahāyaḥ an epithet of Śiva.
— aśanī the full-moon day in the month of Phālguna (holikā).
— āśaḥ fire; pradakṣiṇīkṛtya hutaṃ hutāśaṃ  R. 2. 71. 
— jātavedas a. one who has made an oblation to fire.
— bhuj m. fire; naiśasyārcirhutabhuja iva cchinnabhūyiṣṭhadhūmā  V. 1. 7 ;  U. 5. 9.  ˚priyā Svāhā the wife of Agni.
— vahaḥ fire; janākīrṇaṃ manye hutavahaparītaṃ gṛhamiva  Ś. 5. 10 ; śītāṃśustapano hito hutavahaḥ  Gīt. 9 ;  Me. 43 ;  Rs. 1. 27. 
— homaḥ a Brāhmaṇa who has offered oblations to fire.
— (maṃ) a burnt offering.
